---
title: "Truss Stiffness Method"
---

# The Stiffness Method for Trusses

This site provides resources for learning and applying the **stiffness method** (also known as the direct stiffness method) to truss structures.

Use the navigation above to explore:

- **Theory** — An overview ofthe stiffness method, beginning at member equations derived from Hooke's law, integrating them into a common system of equations, and then discussing how to solve for unknown displcements, reactions, and member forces.
- **Interactive Solver** — A hands-on example where you can select a truss, assign properties and loads, and step through each stage of the solution.

These materials are designed to complement Brigham Young University's CE 321 (Structural Analysis) course. 
If you are new to the stiffness method, you may find it helpful to review the theory section and the interactive solver concurrently.
